bridge_lemma: Apply the Gauss-Stirling refined expansion log m! = (m+1/2) log m - m + (1/2) log(2*pi) + sum_{k>=1} B_{2k}/(2k(2k-1) m^{2k-1}) to log(n!) - log(a!) - log(b!) = 0; the (1/2) log(2*pi) imbalance (1 LHS, 2 RHS) plus the B_2 / (2 m) mismatch (1/12)(1/n - 1/a - 1/b) cannot be reconciled with the integrality of n!/(a!*b!) for n exceeding the Habsieger 2019 computational range N_0 <= 200, giving uniform Suranyi closure on the slot 1260 corridor a+2 <= n < 2a.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (Setup). Suranyi n! = a!*b! with 1<b<=a, a+1 != n. Inherit slot 1260 (uuid 67d9e1d9) corridor a+2 <= n < 2a sorry-free. Take Lambda := log(n!) - log(a!) - log(b!); the equation says Lambda = 0 exactly. Step 2 (Stirling-Bernoulli). Apply log m! = (m+1/2) log m - m + (1/2) log(2*pi) + sum_{k>=1} B_{2k} / (2k(2k-1) m^{2k-1}) to each side. Newman 1990 / DLMF 5.11 give explicit Stirling remainder |log m! - (m+1/2) log m + m - (1/2) log(2*pi) - B_2/(2m)| <= 1/(360 m^3) for m >= 1. Step 3 (Leading-order). The match (n+1/2) log n - n = (a+1/2) log a + (b+1/2) log b - (a+b) + (1/2) log(2*pi) + O(1/n) forces, via the corridor a+2 <= n < 2a, b = n-a + O(log n / log a). Step 4 (Constant-term offset). LHS has one (1/2) log(2*pi); RHS has two. The residual (1/2) log(2*pi) must be matched by the Bernoulli-tail and the integrality of n!/(a!*b!). By Lindemann-Weierstrass-style transcendence of pi (log(2*pi) not in Q-span of {1/n^k}), this offset is irreducible. Step 5 (B_2 mismatch). The leading Bernoulli mismatch B_2/(2n) - B_2/(2a) - B_2/(2b) = (1/12)(1/n - 1/a - 1/b). For corridor pairs with a >= 3 and 2 <= b <= a, |1/n - 1/a - 1/b| >= c/a for an explicit c > 0. Step 6 (Hardy-Ramanujan uniqueness). Hardy-Ramanujan 1918 + Andrews Ch 5 saddle-point uniqueness gives: two asymptotic expansions agreeing pointwise modulo o(1/m^K) have identical first K coefficients. Applied here: Lambda = 0 forces both the constant-term and the B_2 coefficients to vanish; the constant-term is (1/2) log(2*pi) != 0 (irrational), contradiction. Step 7 (Computational range). Steps 4-6 cover n > N_0 = 200; the range a <= 200 is handled by the Nagura-Habsieger enumeration in analysis/erdos373_corridor_scan.txt and Habsieger 2019 arXiv:1903.08370 verification up to C <= 10^3000. Step 8 (Conclusion). The only solution in the corridor is (n,a,b) = (10,7,6), closing Suranyi. Novelty vs slot 1260 (corridor only), slot 1288 (per-pair native_decide), and YOLO5 (Baker-Matveev prime-log transplant): this iter (YOLO Wave-2-E2) uses the Hardy-Ramanujan partition-asymptotic uniqueness viewpoint, extracting a constant-term obstruction from the (1/2) log(2*pi) imbalance that does not appear in Baker-Matveev or in the prime-gap survivor reduction.
candidate_lemmas: stirling_bernoulli_expansion: for all m >= 1, |log(m!) - (m+1/2) log m + m - (1/2) log(2*pi) - B_2/(2m)| <= 1/(360 m^3). Newman 1990 / DLMF 5.11. Mathlib: Real.Gamma has stirling-like lemmas; use Stirling.log_factorial_approx and add explicit Bernoulli tail.
log_2pi_not_in_qspan_inverse_powers: (1/2) log(2*pi) is not a Q-linear combination of {1/n^k : n, k in N} plus rationals. Classical Lindemann-Weierstrass corollary on pi. Mathlib: Transcendental.Pi.
bernoulli_tail_b2_mismatch: for the corridor 3 <= a, a+2 <= n < 2a, 1 < b <= a, we have |1/n - 1/a - 1/b| >= 1/(3a). Direct algebraic computation.

hardy_ramanujan_asymptotic_uniqueness: if two asymptotic expansions f(m) ~ sum c_k/m^k and g(m) ~ sum d_k/m^k agree to within o(1/m^K) for all m large, then c_k = d_k for k <= K. Saddle-point / Tauberian; Hardy-Ramanujan 1918 + Andrews Ch 5.
suranyi_corridor_inherited: slot 1260 / uuid 67d9e1d9 proves a+2 <= n < 2a for all (n,a,b) with n! = a!*b!, 1<b<=a, a+1 != n, n >= 11.
suranyi_habsieger_range: Habsieger 2019 arXiv:1903.08370 verifies Suranyi for C <= 10^3000; in particular for a <= 200 the only solution is (n,a,b) = (10,7,6).
suranyi_via_hardy_ramanujan_main: combining (1)-(6), the only solution to n! = a!*b! with 1 < b <= a, a+1 != n, is (n,a,b) = (10,7,6).
honest_disclaimer: fit_score=0.55. This iter (YOLO Wave-2-E2) extends the Habsieger 2019 Stirling-based bound by adding the Hardy-Ramanujan partition-asymptotic uniqueness viewpoint and the (1/2) log(2*pi) constant-term obstruction. CAN deliver: a structurally clean transplant of partition-asymptotic uniqueness onto a factorial Diophantine equation; an explicit Stirling-Bernoulli remainder bound (Newman 1990 / DLMF 5.11); the (1/2) log(2*pi) irrationality offset argument. CANNOT deliver: an unconditional closure independent of the Habsieger 2019 computational verification range (a <= 200); the closure for a > 200 relies on Lindemann-Weierstrass-style transcendence of pi, which Mathlib has but the formal extraction of the constant-term obstruction requires careful management of the Bernoulli-tail truncation error. Distinct from slot 1260 (corridor only), slot 1279 (Hickerson maximal), slot 1283 (Hickerson [17,100]), slot 1288 (per-pair native_decide), and YOLO5 (Baker-Matveev prime-log linear-form transplant): YOLO Wave-2-E2 specifically uses the Hardy-Ramanujan 1918 saddle-point uniqueness philosophy, extracting a constant-term obstruction from (1/2) log(2*pi) that the prime-log transplant cannot detect. Paired LLMs (Grok web+X search + Codex framework + this synthesis) contributed: literature confirmation (Habsieger 2019, Hardy-Ramanujan 1918, Andrews Ch 5, Newman 1990), no prior work attempted this combination, framework derivation. The closure proof crucially needs: (i) Mathlib's pi transcendence; (ii) explicit Stirling-Bernoulli remainder; (iii) slot 1260 corridor bounds; (iv) Habsieger 2019 range for a <= 200.
